sprint: Platform Hardening — Draft Asset Architecture, Phase D: migration activation
task: Wires the already-built AssetStore.migrateFieldsOnSave (Phase A) into both surfaces' own existing debounced save paths, so content already saved before Phase B/C shipped -- still holding raw embedded data: URIs -- starts shrinking to durable vihu-asset: references one save at a time, with no proactive sweep and no version flag: format-detection alone decides whether a field needs migrating. In js/projectManager.js, a new _collectMigrationAccessors() walks every known image-bearing field on AppState.slides (slide._imageDataURL, every extra Place's own placeContent[id].dataURL, every World-owned Scene Object override's own elementOverrides[id].image) and returns {get,set} accessor pairs; a new _scheduleAssetMigration(), debounced 1500ms and called from _writeStorage()'s own success branch, calls AssetStore.migrateFieldsOnSave('creator', id, accessors) in the background and, only if anything actually changed, performs one more _writeStorage() so the smaller payload actually reaches localStorage. slide.thumbnail is deliberately NOT included -- a disclosed scope decision, not an oversight: several of its own read sites (js/app.js's page-strip/thumbnail-grid renders, js/creatorProjectStore.js/js/magicCardUI.js's own Story/My-Projects cards) were never rewired in Phase C to resolve a reference, and thumbnails are small, derived, regenerable data (ThumbnailEngine) nowhere near the scale that caused the original quota bug, so migrating it now would produce a genuinely broken thumbnail rather than a meaningful storage win. A real, if narrow, race was found and closed at its root, in the shared js/assetStore.js module itself (benefiting World Builder's own identical wiring too): migrateFieldsOnSave's own put()-then-set() step now does a compare-and-swap -- it only rewrites a field if it STILL holds the exact bytes just migrated at the moment the put() resolves, so a field genuinely replaced by a fresh, unrelated upload while the migration's own IndexedDB write was still in flight is left completely untouched rather than silently reverted to a stale reference; verified via a direct, fully-timing-controlled unit-level proof against the real (unmodified) function. A real, pre-existing Phase C read-side gap was also found and fixed in the same pass, unrelated to migration itself: js/pageDesigner.js's Cover/Hook/End Image Manager panel's own preview assigned s._imageDataURL directly with no resolve step -- fixed by reusing s.image.src (the already-loaded, already-resolved Image object the same branch's own condition already confirms exists), rather than adding a second async resolve path. Verified via a new 24-assertion Playwright suite spanning both surfaces (World Builder v2: a Project hand-seeded with legacy data: URIs -- a top-level Identity asset, a standalone Scene Layer image, and an Experience+its mirrored Layer sharing one legacy image -- reopened after a real reload, a real unrelated edit triggers _persist(), every field migrates to a real vihu-asset:builder: reference resolving to a real blob: URL, the mirrored Layer and its source Experience share the identical reference confirming no double upload, a second later save is a genuine no-op; Creator: slide._imageDataURL/placeContent/elementOverrides all migrate correctly through the analogous fix, while slide.thumbnail is confirmed to deliberately stay untouched; plus the compare-and-swap race-fix proof) plus full regression across the existing Phase A/B/C Playwright suites and both goldenBuild.js suites (World Builder v1 and v2), all passing unchanged. No cache-bust bump was needed for root index.html (its own script tags carry no ?v= convention, unchanged since no CSS changed this phase); tools/world-builder-v2/index.html's own ?v= was bumped separately. Phase E (full end-to-end verification, docs, cleanup) remains its own separately-scoped, not-yet-started increment.
